en: And after five days the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a spokesman, one Tertullus. They laid before the governor their case against Paul.
zh: 過了五天，大祭司亞拿尼亞同幾個長老，和一個律師帖土羅來了，他們向總督控告保羅。

en: And when he had been summoned, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying: "Since through you we enjoy much peace, and since by your foresight, most excellent Felix, reforms are being made for this nation,
zh: 保羅傳來了之後，帖土羅就控訴他說：“腓力斯大人，因著你的緣故，我們大大地享受著太平；因著你的遠見，本國大事改革；

en: in every way and everywhere we accept this with all gratitude.
zh: 我們隨時隨地感激不盡。

en: But, to detain you no further, I beg you in your kindness to hear us briefly.
zh: 現在我不想多煩擾你，只求你寬容一下，聽我們講幾句話。

en: For we have found this man a plague, one who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the world and is a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes.
zh: 我們看這個人就像瘟疫一樣，是煽動普天下猶太人生亂的人，又是拿撒勒派的首領。

en: He even tried to profane the temple, but we seized him.
zh: 他還意圖污穢聖殿，我們就把他捉住。

en: By examining him yourself you will be able to find out from him about everything of which we accuse him."
zh: 你親自審問之後，就必清楚知道我們告他的一切事了。”

en: The Jews also joined in the charge, affirming that all these things were so.
zh: 猶太人也都跟他一同控告保羅，證實事情確是這樣。

en: And when the governor had nodded to him to speak, Paul replied: "Knowing that for many years you have been a judge over this nation, I cheerfully make my defense.
zh: 總督向保羅示意，叫他說話，他就說：“我知道你在本國審案多年，所以樂意為自己申辯。

en: You can verify that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship in Jerusalem,
zh: 你清楚知道，自從我上耶路撒冷去禮拜，到現在還沒有十二天；

en: and they did not find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd, either in the temple or in the synagogues or in the city.
zh: 無論在殿裡、會堂中或城裡，他們都沒有看見我跟人辯論，或煽動群眾作亂，

en: Neither can they prove to you what they now bring up against me.
zh: 也不能向你證明他們現在控告我的事。

en: But this I confess to you, that according to the Way, which they call a sect, I worship the God of our fathers, believing everything laid down by the Law and written in the Prophets,
zh: 但有一件事我要向你承認，他們所稱為異端的這道，我正是根據這道來敬拜我祖先的 神的。一切律法和先知所記的，我都相信。

en: having a hope in God, which these men themselves accept, that there will be a resurrection of both the just and the unjust.
zh: 我靠著 神所存的盼望，也是他們自己所期待的，就是義人和不義的人都要復活；

en: So I always take pains to have a clear conscience toward both God and man.
zh: 因此，我常常勉勵自己，對 神對人要常存無虧的良心。

en: Now after several years I came to bring alms to my nation and to present offerings.
zh: 過了幾年我帶著捐款回來賙濟本國，同時也帶了祭物，

en: While I was doing this, they found me purified in the temple, without any crowd or tumult. But some Jews from Asia-
zh: 他們看見我在殿裡的時候，我已經行完了潔淨禮，並沒有和一大群人在一起，也沒有生亂，

en: they ought to be here before you and to make an accusation, should they have anything against me.
zh: 只有幾個從亞西亞來的猶太人而已。他們若有事要控告我，就應該到你面前來控告。

en: Or else let these men themselves say what wrongdoing they found when I stood before the council,
zh: 要不然，當我站在公議會中受審的時候，這些人若發現我有甚麼罪行，早就親自說出來了。

en: other than this one thing that I cried out while standing among them: 'It is with respect to the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial before you this day.'"
zh: 如果有的話，就是我站在他們中間所喊的：‘我今天在你們面前受審，是為了死人復活的事’那一句話。”

en: But Felix, having a rather accurate knowledge of the Way, put them off, saying, "When Lysias the tribune comes down, I will decide your case."
zh: 腓力斯本來就詳細曉得這道，卻故意拖延他們，說：“等千夫長呂西亞來了，再斷定你們的事。”

en: Then he gave orders to the centurion that he should be kept in custody but have some liberty, and that none of his friends should be prevented from attending to his needs.
zh: 於是吩咐百夫長看守保羅，但要寬待他，不可阻止親友來照料他。

en: After some days Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and he sent for Paul and heard him speak about faith in Christ Jesus.
zh: 過了幾天，腓力斯和他的猶太妻子土西拉一同來到，他又傳見保羅，聽他談論在耶穌基督裡的信仰。

en: And as he reasoned about righteousness and self-control and the coming judgment, Felix was alarmed and said, "Go away for the present. When I get an opportunity I will summon you."
zh: 保羅講到公義、自制和將來的審判的時候，腓力斯就害怕起來，說：“你先走吧，等我有空的時候再叫你來。”

en: At the same time he hoped that money would be given him by Paul. So he sent for him often and conversed with him.
zh: 同時他也希望保羅送錢給他，所以又多次傳見他，和他談話。

en: When two years had elapsed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. And desiring to do the Jews a favor, Felix left Paul in prison.
zh: 過了兩年，波求．非斯都接了腓力斯的任；腓力斯為要討好猶太人，就把保羅留在監裡。
